name: user_id
description: Unique ID of the customer or user account.

name: user_name
description: The customer's name as shown on the account.

name: email
description: The email address saved on the customer account. It can also be used to look up the user.

name: phone
description: The phone number saved on the customer account. It can also be used to look up the user.

name: default_address
description: The default shipping address saved on the customer account.

name: default_payment_method_id
description: The ID of the payment method set as the user's default choice. This is the preferred payment method on file, not necessarily the one actually used for a particular payment.

name: product_id
description: Unique ID of the product. This is the SPU-level product record, not a specific size or color option.

name: product_name
description: The product name shown to shoppers.

name: base_price
description: The product's base price before any order-specific pricing is applied.

name: variant_id
description: Unique ID of a specific product variant, such as one color and size combination. Unlike product_id, this points to the exact SKU being bought.

name: color
description: The color of the product variant.

name: size
description: The size of the product variant.

name: order_draft_id
description: Unique ID of an order draft, meaning the shopper's in-progress cart or pre-checkout order before it becomes a real order.

name: draft_status
description: The current state of the order draft, such as still being edited, waiting to be submitted, or expired.

name: updated_time
description: The most recent time the order draft was updated.

name: order_draft_item_id
description: Unique ID of a line item inside an order draft. This is the draft-side item ID before checkout, not the final order_item_id created after the order is placed. Within a draft, the pair (order_draft_id, variant_id) can also uniquely identify the same draft item. When this field appears on an order item, it points back to the draft item that the order line came from.

name: draft_quantity
description: How many units the shopper currently wants for this draft line item before the order is placed.

name: proposed_unit_price
description: The estimated per-item price shown for this draft line item before the order is finalized.

name: pricing_snapshot_id
description: Unique ID of a saved pricing result for an order draft. It represents the quote used for the draft at a particular moment before checkout. If the draft is later submitted as an order, this is the pricing snapshot that the order points back to.

name: snapshot_status
description: The current state of the pricing snapshot, such as valid, expired, or pending confirmation.

name: quoted_total_amount
description: The total amount quoted to the shopper in that pricing snapshot. This is the overall quoted total for the draft at that moment.

name: order_id
description: Unique ID of the final placed order. Unlike order_draft_id, this refers to the real order after checkout.

name: order_status
description: The current state of the placed order, such as placed, paid, shipped, delivered, or canceled. This is for the final order, not the draft.

name: created_time
description: The time when the final order was created after checkout.

name: order_item_id
description: Unique ID of a line item in the final placed order. Unlike order_draft_item_id, this is the order-side item record created after checkout. Within an order, the pair (order_id, variant_id) can also uniquely identify the same order line.

name: purchased_quantity
description: How many units were actually recorded on the final order line. Unlike draft_quantity, this is the quantity on the placed order.

name: unit_price
description: The per-item price recorded on the final order line. Unlike proposed_unit_price, this is the price that ended up on the placed order.

name: payment_method_id
description: Unique ID of a saved payment method. This identifies the payment instrument itself, such as a card or account, not a payment attempt or payment record.

name: payment_method_type
description: The kind of payment method, such as a credit card, debit card, or digital wallet.

name: account_number
description: The stored or masked account number for the payment method, such as a card number or its visible last few digits. It can also be used to identify the payment method.

name: payment_intent_id
description: Unique ID of the payment intent for an order. This represents the stage where the system is preparing to start a payment, not the final payment result.

name: intent_status
description: The current state of the payment intent at the intent stage, such as pending confirmation, waiting for authorization, or canceled. This is earlier than the later authorization and payment records.

name: auth_id
description: Unique ID of the authorization record for a payment attempt. This is the step where the payment processor approves or declines the charge, and it sits between payment_intent_id and payment_id.

name: auth_status
description: The current state of the authorization step, such as approved, declined, or waiting for additional verification.

name: auth_code
description: The authorization code or other authorization credential returned by the payment processor.

name: payment_id
description: Unique ID of the recorded payment itself. Unlike payment_intent_id or auth_id, this is the actual payment record after the payment flow reaches that stage.

name: payment_status
description: The current state of the payment record, such as paid, failed, reversed, or refunded. This is the status of the actual payment, not just the intent or auth step.

name: payment_time
description: The time when the payment was completed.

name: warehouse_request_id
description: Unique ID of the warehouse request for one fulfillment group of an order. This means the order has entered warehouse or fulfillment processing. The same order can have multiple warehouse requests.

name: warehouse_request_status
description: The current state of that warehouse request in the fulfillment process, such as queued, picking, packed, or canceled.

name: warehouse_request_time
description: The time when that warehouse request was initiated.

name: shipment_id
description: Unique ID of the shipment record created for a warehouse request. In this simplified setup, one warehouse request corresponds to one shipment.

name: shipment_status
description: The current state of that shipment, such as label created, in transit, delivered, or exception.

name: tracking_number
description: The tracking number for that shipment.

name: delivery_attempt_id
description: Unique ID of a specific delivery attempt for a shipment. One shipment can have multiple delivery attempts, so this is narrower than shipment_id.

name: attempt_status
description: The current state of that specific delivery attempt, such as out for delivery, delivery failed, or delivered. This is for one attempt, not the whole shipment.

name: attempt_time
description: The time when that delivery attempt happened.

name: return_request_id
description: Unique ID of the customer's return request for an order. This is the initial 'I want to return this order' step. In this simplified setup, a returned order has one return request.

name: return_status
description: The current state of the return request for that order.

name: requested_time
description: The time when the customer initiated that return request.

name: return_audit_id
description: Unique ID of the internal review record for a return request. This is the audit step where the platform or merchant reviews the return. In this simplified setup, each return request has one audit record.

name: audit_status
description: The current state or outcome of that return audit, such as pending review, approved, or rejected.

name: refund_id
description: Unique ID of the refund record. This is the final refund-layer record created only after a return audit is approved, linking that approved return to the original payment record. In this simplified setup, one refund record is tied to one return audit and one payment record. Only orders that actually have a refund will have this field.

name: refund_status
description: The current state of the refund. This field appears only when a return audit has been approved and an actual refund record exists.

name: refund_amount
description: The amount of money returned in the refund. This field appears only when a return audit has been approved and an actual refund record exists.

name: refund_time
description: The time when the refund was made. This field appears only when a return audit has been approved and an actual refund record exists.
